name: sql_select
summary: Executes a SQL SELECT query and emits each result row as a message. Supports PostgreSQL, SQLite, MySQL, and others.

driver.description: Database driver. Use "postgres" for PostgreSQL, "sqlite" for SQLite.

table.description: Table (or view) name to select from.

columns.description: Columns to select. Use ["*"] to select all.

where.description: Optional SQL WHERE clause (without the WHERE keyword), e.g. "created_at > ?".

args_mapping.description: Bloblang mapping that produces an array of values to bind to WHERE placeholders.

prefix.description: SQL fragment inserted before the SELECT statement.

suffix.description: SQL fragment appended after the SELECT statement (e.g. "ORDER BY id LIMIT 100").

init_statement.description: SQL statement executed once when the connection is established (e.g. PRAGMA or SET commands).
